analysis.title: Game Report

descriptions.version: The Chess engine that will be analysing your moves.
descriptions.depth: The number of moves that the engine looks ahead when analysing moves.
descriptions.lines: The number of variations that the engine will consider. Must be at least 2 for some classifications.
descriptions.timeLimit: The maximum amount of time the engine is allowed to analyse a single move.
descriptions.threadCount: The number of threads to run the engine with. Optimally this will be the number of CPU cores you have available.

suggestionArrows.disabled: Disabled: Do not show engine arrows
suggestionArrows.continuation: Top Continuation: Show an arrow for the top move in this position
suggestionArrows.alternative: Top Alternative: Show an arrow for the top move in the previous position

descriptions.simpleNotation: Show simple descriptions of Chess moves instead of standard notation. Example: Kxf4 -> King on e3 takes f4

gameSearchMenu.title: Select a game

gameSearchMenu.userNotFound: We couldn't find an account with this username.
gameSearchMenu.noGamesFound: We couldn't find any games for this month.

progressReporter.classifyFailed: Unknown error while generating the game report; try refreshing the page.

classifiedMoveCard.insufficientLines: Make sure the previous move is fully evaluated for a classification.

analysis.title: Analysing a game
analysis.part1: You can analyse your Chess games on the
analysis.part3: . Stockfish evaluations, accuracy percentages and move classifications - so that you can find your brilliancies and blunders - are provided free of charge. You will find an analysis board in the centre of the page, which is where you can view the moves of the game you have selected. To select a game, you need to use the game selector we have provided on the right-hand sidebar. It looks like this:
analysis.part4: You can enter any game using its PGN. If you would like, you can also select a game from your Chess.com or Lichess account if you provide your username. You will need to click the "Search Games" button to browse the games on your account, and can select any of them just by clicking them. Lastly, you can enter a single FEN position. In any case, you will be able to add moves manually after you import a game, which will all be evaluated and classified on the fly.
analysis.part5: After you've selected a game, just click the "Analyse" button to begin the analysis. You can also just move a piece on the board if you'd like to begin from the starting position.

bugReporting.title: Bug Reporting
bugReporting.bugReportingModeDescription: Help us find and fix bugs. Bug Reporting Mode activates a small widget in the bottom corner of your screen which you can click to copy console logs.
